stories.headline: DeFi TVL Craters $13B in 48 Hours as Kelp Contagion Spirals Beyond Aave
stories.tldr: Total value locked across DeFi fell from $99.5B to $86.3B in two days, turning the Kelp exploit into a sector-wide confidence crisis.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Contagion jumped from a single bridge to the entire lending stack — pricing in tail risk for cross-chain collateral everywhere, not just restaking.
the_details: DeFi TVL dropped 13.3% from $99.497B to $86.286B between Apr 18 and Apr 20 per CoinDesk data.
Aave absorbed the bulk, shedding $8.45B to land at $17.947B as rsETH holders rushed exits.
DeFi logged 47 incidents in the first 4.5 months of 2026, a 68% YoY rise over 2025's pace.

stories.headline: Aave Pools Hit 100% Utilization as $300M Emergency Borrowing Spike Traps Lenders
stories.tldr: A $300M borrowing surge pushed Aave's ETH, USDT and USDC pools to full utilization, locking depositors out as whales pulled $6B in 24 hours.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Full utilization isn't a liquidity warning — it is the event. Depositors can't withdraw and borrowers owe a spiking rate until someone blinks.
the_details: Whales drained more than $6B from Aave in the 24 hours after the Kelp exploit per on-chain data.
Stranded users borrowed roughly $300M against their own locked stablecoins at punitive rates.
Major pools — ETH, USDT, USDC — hit 100% utilization, freezing exits until rates attract fresh supply.

stories.headline: April 2026 Is Crypto's Worst Hack Month Since Feb 2025: $606M Stolen in 18 Days
stories.tldr: Crypto protocols have lost over $606M to exploits in just 18 days of April, making it the worst month for theft since February 2025.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Back-to-back nine-figure attacks on Drift and Kelp reset insurance pricing assumptions and give regulators a live case for tighter bridge rules.
the_details: Drift, a Solana perpetuals protocol, was drained for ~$285M early April in an attack linked to DPRK actors.
Kelp DAO lost $292M on Apr 19 via a LayerZero cross-chain verification spoof releasing 116,500 rsETH.
At least a dozen smaller protocols have been hit in the weeks between, per on-chain trackers.

stories.headline: Crypto Week Ahead: U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Expires Midweek, Tesla Earnings in Focus
stories.tldr: Markets open the week jittery as the U.S.-Iran ceasefire is set to expire midweek and Tesla earnings frame risk appetite for crypto.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Oil and geopolitics are back as BTC's dominant macro driver — a reopening Strait rallies risk, a flare-up sends it the other way just as DeFi is already wounded.
the_details: U.S. seized an Iranian-flagged tanker Sunday after Iran fired at ships attempting the Strait of Hormuz.
The CoinDesk 20 is down 3.6% since Friday 4pm ET, led by AAVE (-22.9%) and ICP (-7.9%).
Tesla reports earnings midweek — a barometer for risk-on appetite after weeks of Mag-7 weakness.

stories.headline: Whales Stack $24M in AAVE Longs Betting DeFi Bluechip Bounces Off Panic Lows
stories.tldr: Whales opened nearly $24M in high-leverage AAVE longs, positioning for a rebound after the token shed 22.9% over the weekend.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Smart money is fading the panic — a signal that the Kelp contagion may be priced in if Aave's bad-debt math holds.
the_details: AAVE led CoinDesk 20 laggards with a 22.9% weekend drop, deepest in the index.
Whales opened roughly $24M in longs across perps venues, per public positioning data.
Aave TVL still sits at $17.9B — enough dry powder to make it the default DeFi rebound trade.
